Group work builds peer learning, accountability, and discussion based depth. One on one work builds individualized pacing and gap filling. Many students benefit from both at the same time.

We offer small group sessions for students who want collaborative practice alongside individual coaching. The combination usually outperforms either approach alone for most subjects.
education.designerNotes: Two scenes in one frame. Small group around a table on one side, one on one on the other.

education.caption:
Parents often ask whether tutoring will create dependency.

The honest answer is the opposite. Good tutoring builds independence by teaching the student how to study, how to organize, and how to seek help productively. The student leaves more capable than they arrived.

We measure success by sessions saved as much as by sessions completed. The goal is for our students to outgrow us.
education.designerNotes: Student walking away from a tutoring desk with confidence, books in hand. Forward facing composition.

beauty.caption:
Color appointments work best when you book the consultation a week ahead.

A fifteen minute consult lets us see your hair in natural light, talk through what you actually want, and source the right product for your specific tone. The day of color appointment goes faster, fits the time we set aside, and the result lands closer to what was in your head.

We always offer the consult free for new clients. The investment in the relationship matters more than the upfront cost.
beauty.designerNotes: Stylist holding color swatches against a client's hair in soft natural light by a window.

beauty.caption:
Extensions are not a quick add on. They are a partnership with the hair you have.

A proper install protects your natural hair, blends in seamlessly, and lasts for months. A rushed install damages the underlying hair, looks obvious from across a room, and turns into a repair job. The difference is technique and the quality of the hair sourced.

We specialize in tape ins and weft installs. We will tell you which method fits your hair, your lifestyle, and your maintenance budget before we book.
beauty.designerNotes: Close up of a clean extension install with seamless blend, side angle of styled hair after.

beauty.caption:
Cuts are an investment that lasts longer than most clients realize.

A precise cut grows out gracefully because each section is shaped to support the next phase. A rushed cut grows out into a problem within four weeks. The same shape, two different lifespans.

We build cuts that are designed to grow well. Many of our clients book six to eight weeks out and the cut still feels intentional in week five.
beauty.designerNotes: Three frame style sequence. Day of cut, week three, week six. Same hair, evolving naturally.

beauty.caption:
Bridal hair and makeup is a full team operation.

We do trial runs four to six weeks before the wedding so the timeline, the look, and the products all get tested in advance. The day of, we send a stylist team that handles the bride and the bridal party in parallel. Everyone is camera ready before first look.

We book bridal teams six to eight months in advance. If you are getting married next year, send us your date now.
beauty.designerNotes: Bride in chair with stylist working, soft glamour lighting, getting ready environment.

beauty.caption:
Lash and brow services are the small upgrades that change how every photo looks.

A lash lift, a brow lamination, a tint and shape. Each one takes less than an hour and the result lasts six to eight weeks. The morning routine gets shorter, the makeup goes on faster, and the eyes look like a more rested version of themselves.

We offer them as add ons to most appointments because clients keep asking. The value to time ratio is one of the best in the salon.
beauty.designerNotes: Eye level macro of beautifully shaped brows and lifted lashes. Editorial styling.

beauty.caption:
Skin care services are a bridge between salon services and a clinical aesthetician.

A hydrating facial, a chemical peel at the right strength, an extraction session done correctly. Each one supports the rest of your beauty routine. We refer to clinical providers when the work calls for it and we deliver maintenance level care in house.

The right cadence is usually every four to six weeks. Most of our clients book recurring slots and notice the cumulative effect.
beauty.designerNotes: Aesthetician applying a serum during a facial in a calm dim treatment room.

beauty.caption:
Education forward stylists do better work and we hire only that kind.

Every stylist on our team takes at least two continuing education classes a year. New techniques, new color lines, new chemistry. The industry evolves and the best stylists evolve with it.

It is part of why our pricing reflects what it does. Skill takes time to maintain and we honor the time.
beauty.designerNotes: Stylist at a continuing education class with model and instructor, focused workshop setting.

beauty.caption:
Booking online is faster than calling and we set the system up to be useful.

Real time availability across every stylist. Service descriptions. Approximate timing. Cancellation policy. Reminder texts a day before so nobody forgets.

If you have a complex request, the call is still better. For routine appointments, the online tool gets you scheduled in under a minute and you can rebook from your couch.
beauty.designerNotes: Phone showing booking interface with calendar visible. Clean UI mockup style.

beauty.caption:
Color correction is a multi visit process and the timeline matters.

If you came in with box dye to fix, or with a color from another stylist that did not land, the work usually takes two to three sessions to do safely. We take the time because forcing it in one session damages the hair and limits where the color can go from there.

We map the plan in the consult, share the timeline, and stick to it. Patience is part of the result.
beauty.designerNotes: Three frame progression of color correction. Before, mid process, after. Honest visible progress.

beauty.caption:
We carry product lines in the salon for a reason and a discount is part of it.

The shampoo and conditioner you bought from a drugstore are formulated differently than the ones we use at the chair. Sometimes the difference is dramatic. Other times it is marginal. We tell you the truth on each one rather than pushing every product on the shelf.

If the salon line is a real upgrade for your hair we will say so. If the drugstore version works fine we will say that too.
beauty.designerNotes: Salon shelf with select products, stylist's hand picking one. Editorial product styling.

beverage.caption:
The taproom rotation refreshes Friday and we are pretty excited about this lineup.

Three returning seasonals, two new brews from our pilot system, and a barrel aged release that has been resting since the spring. Tap list updates live on the website by noon.

We do flights at the bar for anyone who wants to taste through. Tell the bartender what styles you usually like and they will pick four for you to try.
beverage.designerNotes: Tap handles in a row with the new list visible behind, low light bar setting, beer in hand.

beverage.caption:
Brewery tours run Saturday afternoons and they are more fun than they sound on paper.

Forty five minutes through the brew house with our head brewer, a couple of small pours along the way, and a chance to ask the questions you would not get to ask at the bar. We cap each tour at twelve people on purpose.

Tours are free with reservation. Walk ins join the next available tour or grab a drink in the taproom while you wait.
beverage.designerNotes: Head brewer pointing at the mash tun with a small group listening, warm industrial setting.

beverage.caption:
Private events at the brewery work for groups from twelve to eighty.

We have hosted rehearsal dinners, milestone birthdays, company holiday parties, and one engagement party with a custom beer the couple helped brew. The space is part industrial, part neighborhood, fully welcoming.

If you have a date in mind, send it to us with a head count. We will send back a quote, a beverage plan, and food partner options the same day.
beverage.designerNotes: Long table set up for a private event in the taproom with string lights overhead.

beverage.caption:
Cellar releases are our favorite project and the limited bottles go fast.

We age select beers in barrels for six to eighteen months. The result is a small batch release that is unlike anything we have on tap day to day. Funky, deep, sometimes weird, always worth the wait.

The next bottle release drops in two weeks. Members get first access twenty four hours before public sale. The members list closes the day before each release.
beverage.designerNotes: Bottles lined up in a cool cellar, light angle catching the labels. Editorial product feel.

beverage.caption:
Our outdoor patio is dog friendly and family friendly and it is one of our favorite parts of the brewery.

String lights, communal tables, food truck most weekends, and enough space for the kids to run around safely. It is the kind of place where someone you have never met before strikes up a conversation about whatever is on tap.

The patio opens for the season this week. Wave to the dog at the next table, they would love a friend.
beverage.designerNotes: Patio scene with families, dogs, and string lights at golden hour. Honest joyful energy.

beverage.caption:
Food truck rotation lands every Friday and Saturday.

This week we have a tacos truck on Friday and a wood fired pizza truck on Saturday. Both are local favorites we book back to back through the season because the lines tell us so.

We post the schedule a week ahead on the website and the social channels. Plan your taproom visit around the food and bring an appetite.
beverage.designerNotes: Food truck parked at the brewery exterior with patrons grabbing food. Casual joyful scene.

beverage.caption:
Beer education classes run quarterly and they fill up fast.

A two hour evening class taught by our head brewer. We cover ingredients, fermentation, style families, and how to taste beer with a more discerning palate. Includes flights and a small bottle to take home.

The next class is at the end of the month. Twenty seats. Reserve through the website.
beverage.designerNotes: Class scene with brewer pointing at ingredients, attendees with flights in front of them.

beverage.caption:
Flagship beers are the recipes we have committed to long term.

A pilsner, an IPA, a porter, and one rotating wild card. The first three are on every taproom list we run, year round. They get tweaked seasonally as ingredients shift but the core recipes hold steady.

If you have not tried our flagships yet, ask the bartender for the lineup. They are the easiest way to understand what we are about as a brewery.
beverage.designerNotes: Three flagship glasses lined up on the bar with the wild card slot empty. Editorial product.

beverage.caption:
Growlers and crowlers are how the taproom comes home with you.

A growler is sixty four ounces, a crowler is thirty two ounces. Both can be filled with any beer on tap. The growler is reusable and we discount the refill. The crowler is a one trip aluminum can that goes in the recycling.

If you are heading to a friend's place tonight, fill a crowler on the way. It is exactly like having the taproom over for the evening.
beverage.designerNotes: Growler being filled at the tap with the crowler can beside it. Side angle, action feel.

beverage.caption:
We brew with local ingredients whenever the season allows.

Local hops in late summer. Local stone fruit in early fall. A grain partnership with a small farm an hour away. The radius of where our ingredients come from matters and it shapes the beer.

The seasonal release this month uses peaches from a farm we have worked with for three years. Limited run. Available on tap and in cans until it is gone.
beverage.designerNotes: Crate of fresh peaches at the brewery with the brewer inspecting them. Documentary feel.

health.caption:
Hormone testing is one of the most overlooked diagnostic tools in adult health.

Fatigue, sleep issues, weight changes, mood shifts, low libido. These symptoms get attributed to stress more often than they should. A comprehensive hormone panel surfaces patterns that a basic CBC will not catch.

We run hormone panels at the start of every functional medicine intake. The results often explain a year or more of symptoms in a single sit down conversation.
health.designerNotes: Lab report on a clinic desk with key numbers highlighted, calm warm setting.

health.caption:
Gut health affects more than digestion.

Mood, immune function, skin clarity, energy levels. The microbiome is upstream of more body systems than most people realize. We use comprehensive stool testing to identify imbalances and design protocols around what each individual gut actually needs.

If you have ongoing symptoms that have not responded to standard interventions, the gut is often where the conversation should start.
health.designerNotes: Layered diagram of body systems with the gut at the center. Educational illustration style.

health.caption:
IV therapy is a delivery method, not a magic fix.

When used correctly, IV nutrients bypass digestion and reach cells faster than oral supplements. We use IV therapy for specific protocols where the speed and bioavailability matter, not as a wellness trend.

If an IV protocol is right for your case we walk through what it is doing and why. If oral supplementation is sufficient we recommend that instead.
health.designerNotes: IV setup in a calm clinic room with a patient relaxed in a comfortable chair.

health.caption:
Acupuncture has decades of clinical research supporting its use for specific conditions.

Chronic pain, migraine, certain reproductive concerns, post operative recovery. The evidence is meaningful and the side effect profile is among the lowest in medicine.

We integrate acupuncture into our broader functional medicine practice when the case calls for it. Most patients experience the benefits within three to five sessions.
health.designerNotes: Calm acupuncture treatment scene with a soft focus, peaceful environment.

health.caption:
Nutrition counseling is more than a meal plan.

We look at how food interacts with your specific physiology. Inflammation markers, blood sugar response, micronutrient status, food sensitivities. The plan that comes out is targeted to the patient, not pulled from a book.

Most patients see meaningful changes in the metrics they care about within six to eight weeks of consistent application. The plan adapts as the body responds.
health.designerNotes: Top down of fresh ingredients arranged on a clean counter with a notebook beside them.

health.caption:
Functional medicine looks for root causes, not symptom suppression.

A prescription that masks a symptom is sometimes the right answer. Often it is not. The harder, slower work of understanding why a body is producing a symptom usually leads to better outcomes over years.

We spend longer at the first appointment than most clinics do. Sixty to ninety minutes. The depth of the intake is where the real diagnostic work happens.
health.designerNotes: Practitioner taking detailed notes during a long consult, two coffee cups visible. Quiet attentive.

health.caption:
Lab driven personalization beats one size fits all every time.

Omega 3 levels, vitamin D status, B vitamin function markers, inflammation, blood sugar dynamics, micronutrient panels. We use comprehensive labs to design supplementation that matches the person.

Most patients leave their first visit understanding their numbers in plain language and knowing exactly which supplements they actually need versus the ones they have been buying out of habit.
health.designerNotes: Lab values printed and highlighted on a desk, supplements arranged beside them.

health.caption:
Sleep is foundational and we treat it as one of the first interventions.

If sleep is broken, almost no other intervention works as well as it should. Hormones, blood sugar, mood, recovery, immune function. All compromised by chronic insufficient sleep.

We work with patients on sleep hygiene, evening routines, light exposure, and targeted supplements when appropriate. Most patients see measurable improvements within two weeks of focused work on this layer.
health.designerNotes: Calm bedroom at twilight with a journal and a glass of water on the nightstand.

health.caption:
Chronic stress shows up in lab values long before patients describe it as stress.

Elevated morning cortisol, inverted diurnal patterns, suppressed thyroid output, blood sugar instability. The body keeps a record even when the mind tells itself things are fine.

We use a combination of lifestyle protocols, targeted supplementation, and sometimes referrals to therapists when the case warrants. Stress physiology is a real diagnostic and a real intervention point.
health.designerNotes: Cortisol curve graph with healthy versus inverted patterns side by side. Educational.

health.caption:
Most chronic conditions are not single cause, single fix problems.

Digestive issues, autoimmune flares, hormonal imbalances, persistent fatigue. Each has multiple contributing factors and each responds to a layered intervention. There is no single supplement that resolves them.

We build long term care plans that address contributing factors in priority order. Patients see incremental improvements through the process and the gains compound.
health.designerNotes: Stack of clear glass layers with labels at each level, building upward. Conceptual visual.

retail.caption:
New arrivals land Thursday and the dressing rooms book up fast.

A fresh capsule from one of our favorite small designers, a few summer linens that finally arrived after the supply chain delay, and a jewelry restock that includes some of the pieces that sold out in spring.

We hold styling appointments throughout the week. Tell us what you are looking for and we will pull a fitting room before you arrive.
retail.designerNotes: Editorial flat lay of new arrivals on a clean wooden table. Soft natural light.

retail.caption:
Personal styling appointments are the way to actually use a closet you already own.

We come over for a closet edit before we recommend anything new. Often the gaps you think are missing pieces are actually styling combinations you have not tried.

The consult is free for clients who book a styling session in store. Bring a few looks and we will work backward from your wardrobe.
retail.designerNotes: Open closet with curated outfit pulled and laid out on a bed. Editorial styling.

retail.caption:
Sustainable brands are not a trend on our shelves. They are a buying philosophy.

Every brand we carry has a sourcing standard we have actually verified. Where the materials come from, who makes the garment, how the workers are paid. These criteria narrow the buying universe and we are good with that.

If you ask about any piece on the floor we can tell you where and how it was made. Transparency is part of the value.
retail.designerNotes: Behind the scenes shot of a small brand atelier or maker at work. Documentary editorial feel.

retail.caption:
Gift wrapping is free and we take it seriously.

Heavy paper, ribbon by the spool, dried flower garnish. Whatever you bought looks more meaningful when it leaves the store. We have wrapped engagement gifts, retirement gifts, milestone birthdays, and more reluctant Valentine's purchases than we can count.

The wrap is on the house. The pressure to remember an occasion is on you, but we are happy to help.
retail.designerNotes: Beautifully wrapped package with brand color ribbon and dried flower. Editorial composition.

retail.caption:
Local delivery is one of the small services that makes a big difference.

If you live within a fifteen minute radius and you order before two p m on a weekday, we will deliver the same day. The driver is one of our staff who knows the neighborhoods. The packaging is intentional. The experience feels like a friend dropping something off.

No charge on orders over a hundred. Modest fee otherwise. We made it easy because the alternative was watching purchases sit in inboxes.
retail.designerNotes: Wrapped package being handed over at a doorstep with a hand written tag. Warm honest scene.

retail.caption:
Curated jewelry has changed how our customers think about every piece.

We carry small designers who make in small runs. Many of the pieces are one of one or one of a few. The relationship with the maker is often the difference between a piece you buy in a moment and a piece you keep for decades.

If you see something on the floor that catches you, ask about the maker. We are happy to share the story.
retail.designerNotes: Macro of a single piece of jewelry on a velvet tray with the maker's mark visible.

retail.caption:
Home goods on our shelves are chosen the same way the apparel is.

Small makers, considered materials, pieces we would put in our own homes. A linen napkin set, a hand thrown ceramic vase, a hand woven basket from a co op we have worked with for years.

If you are setting up a new space or refreshing a corner of your home, we can help with curation. The home section is small on purpose.
retail.designerNotes: Styled home goods vignette with a vase, linen, ceramics. Editorial home magazine feel.

retail.caption:
Seasonal sales are intentional and they are not the way we usually price.

We run two real sales a year, end of season, on pieces that are transitioning out of the floor. The discounts are meaningful and the inventory is genuine. We do not run constant promotions because the regular pricing is already considered.

The end of season sale starts in two weeks. Members on the email list get first access on Wednesday morning.
retail.designerNotes: Sale sign in clean typography on a single rack. Editorial restraint, not bargain bin energy.

retail.caption:
Brand stories are why we love this work.

The sweater you tried on yesterday was made in a workshop with seven employees who have been together for ten years. The tote bag was sewn by a co op that supports women returning to work. The jewelry was hand finished by a designer who cares deeply about provenance.

We share the stories on the wall in store and in the captions online. The stories are part of what you take home.
retail.designerNotes: Hand written brand story card next to a piece on a shelf. Tactile, authentic feel.

retail.caption:
Loyalty programs that actually serve customers are rare and we built ours to be one.

No points to track. No tiers to grind toward. A simple rewards model where members get early access to new arrivals, first invites to events, free local delivery, and a birthday note worth using.

Sign up at the register or online. The membership is free. The intent is to deepen the relationship, not to gamify it.
retail.designerNotes: Membership card with brand color in a customer's hand at the register. Warm transactional moment.
